title: session-state.json (v4 on-disk write shape)
description: Machine-readable contract for the v4 session-state.json files the ai_router writers emit (Set 084 S1). Documents the canonical known-plan shape AND the plan-less carve-out (no sessions[] key). Readers stay tolerant of pre-v4 files via the normalize shim; this schema covers what a v4 WRITER may produce. The prose reference is docs/session-state-schema.md; the generated example is docs/session-state-schema-example.json. Parity with the pure-Python writer/validator is pinned by ai_router/tests/test_orchestrator_identity.py::TestSessionStateSchemaParity (L-066-1: both directions).

schemaVersion.description: v4 since Set 047. Older versions are read-normalized, never written.

status.description: Canonical top-level lifecycle state. Aliases (completed/done) are read-tolerated, never written.

sessions.description: The canonical per-session ledger. Absent only in the plan-less carve-out (totalSessions unresolvable at first start).

startedAt.description: Plan-less carve-out passthrough only; the canonical known-plan shape carries startedAt per session.

orchestrator.description: Plan-less carve-out passthrough only; the canonical known-plan shape carries the block per session.

nextOrchestrator.description: Recommendation for the next session (written at close). Shape owned by session_state.NextOrchestrator; not re-specified here.

forceClosed.description: Opaque passthrough set by close_session --force; preserved across rewrites.

preCancelStatus.description: Opaque passthrough captured by cancel_session_set; preserved across rewrites.

status.description: Per-session 'cancelled' is reserved for a future schema and rejected in v4.

type.description: Set 057 typed sessions. Absent means 'work'; the writer persists only non-work values.

verificationVerdict.description: Canonical tokens are VERIFIED / ISSUES_FOUND (plus WAIVED for external verification); operators have shipped extension tokens (e.g. ISSUES_FOUND_RESOLVED_IN_FLIGHT) that readers PREFIX-MATCH, so this is typed string, not an enum. Set 086: the blessed writer (session_state.validate_verification_verdict) fails closed against an EXACT allowlist of canonical + shipped-extension tokens, so a confabulated non-verdict (manual-override-development) or an invented prefix look-alike (VERIFIED_NOT_REALLY) can never persist here. Readers stay lenient; the writer is strict.

orchestratorBlock.description: Omit-null (Set 049 P2): missing keys are valid; null values and 'unknown' placeholders are not. Set 084 (F1): identityProvenance is derived from the engine by the shared writer (session_state.build_orchestrator_block) and is never a free choice. The 'provider' field is a seat DESCRIPTOR; the effective provider is derived at use time by registry lookup on 'model' (ai_router/orchestrator_identity.py). additionalProperties stays true because pre-Set-049 blocks preserved across rewrites may carry retired coordination fields readers tolerate.

identityProvenance.description: Set 084 (F1, additive): how identity was established. 'asserted' for multi-provider engines (Copilot seats), 'direct' for single-vendor engines. Absent on pre-084 blocks.
